entities.name: HIERARCHICAL MULTIPLE-FACTOR ANALYSIS (HMFA)
entities.description: A numerical analysis approach used to combine different types of phenotypic and genetic data to create principal axes for classification.

entities.name: WARD MODIFIED LOCATION MODEL (WARD-MLM)
entities.description: A two-stage classification strategy used in conjunction with HMFA to partition genotypes into homogeneous subpopulations.

entities.name: GENOTYPES
entities.description: The genetic makeup of individual organisms being partitioned into homogeneous subpopulations.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC DATA
entities.description: Observable physical or biochemical characteristics used as variables for classification.

entities.name: GENETIC DATA
entities.description: DNA-based information used as variables for classification.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL AXES
entities.description: New axes derived from HMFA that represent the contribution of original phenotypic and genetic variables.

entities.name: CORE SUBSETS
entities.description: Small, representative groups of genotypes formed for the purpose of conservation.

entities.name: GENETIC RESOURCE CONSERVATION
entities.description: The practice of preserving genetic diversity within a species.

entities.name: CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of partitioning a heterogeneous population of genotypes into non-overlapping homogeneous subpopulations.
